title: UMS <-> IDApTIK game taxonomy map
description: The versioned mapping at the DLC manifest seam between the UMS editor taxonomy (abi/Types.idr DeviceKind, abi/Zones.idr Zone) and the game taxonomy (idaptik-core device::DeviceKind, network::Zone). Device kinds: per the extend-the-enum ruling (docs/adr/0002), the game enum was extended to a superset of the UMS enum, so the map is a total 1:1 identity — kept as an explicit table so drift on either side is caught by scripts/validate_dlc.py and tests/test_taxonomy_map.py, which derive their vocabularies from this file. Zones: UMS zones are {name, securityTier}; the game has 8 named segments. zone-tier-bands is the DEFAULT securityTier -> segment banding (highest min-tier <= tier wins); an authored per-zone 'segment' field on add_zone overrides it. The zone half was RATIFIED by the owner on 2026-07-21 exactly as defaulted (see zone-defaults-status): the tier band ladder stands, Iot/Isp/Service stay override-only, and an authored per-zone segment always wins over the banding.
zone-notes: Rationale for the default bands: tier 0 = public front of house (Lan), 1 = perimeter-exposed (Dmz), 2 = protected interior (Internal), 3 = restricted operations (Management), 4+ = critical plant (Scada). Iot, Isp and Service are functional categories rather than clearance bands, so no tier maps to them by default; a zone reaches them only via an explicit per-zone segment override on add_zone.
